column1: Source of Truth: OSMP-001-UTIL-v20 + OSMP-001-CIP-v15 | Docket: OSMP-001-UTIL (patent pending)
Inventor: Clay Holberg | Filing Date: March 17, 2026
Standard namespaces: 26 (A-Z) | Tier 2: 351 order-invariant double-Latin slots (AB=BA canonical) | Autonomous Ops MDR extension: 26 new opcodes
Sovereign extension: Ω: (U+03A9) | Floor version: 1.0 | All entries floor_version=1.0
SECTION 1: GLYPH OPERATORS — SIX-CATEGORY ARCHITECTURE (UTIL Section X-C)
All glyphs have pre-existing formal definitions predating and independent of OSMP.
CATEGORY 1 — LOGICAL AND COMPOSITIONAL OPERATORS (18 total)

column5: if...then | when...then | provided that | therefore | because
if and only if | iff | exactly when
any | there exists | at least one | some
equals | is set to | value is | namespace-separator (positional)
then next | followed by | in sequence
not equal to | excluding value | except value
in priority order | ranked | strict ranked execution

column1: CATEGORY 2 — CONSEQUENCE CLASS DESIGNATORS (UTIL Section X-C Cat.2; Claims 25 and X-B)
Mandatory structural element in R namespace instruction frames. An R namespace instruction without a valid Cat.2 designator is malformed and non-executable.

column5: Physical instruction with potential for irreversible harm; requires I:§ human operator confirmation as structural precondition to execution
Physical instruction whose execution can be undone or corrected; I:§ not required
Physical instruction producing permanent physical change; requires I:§ human operator confirmation as structural precondition to execution

Human operator confirmation opcode in I namespace (I:§); encodes a human operator confirmation token comprising signature hash + timestamp + TTL; required as precondition for R:⚠ and R:⊘ instructions; 2 UTF-8 bytes

column1: CATEGORY 3 — OUTCOME STATE DESIGNATORS (UTIL Section X-C Cat.3)
Formal logical constants predating OSMP; adopted for pre-existing definitions. Used as Q namespace result values and I namespace verification outcomes.

column5: Logical pass state; outcome of successful I namespace verification workflow
Logical fail state; outcome of failed I namespace verification workflow

column1: CATEGORY 4 — PARAMETER AND SLOT DESIGNATORS (UTIL Section X-C Cat.4)
Pre-existing mathematical meanings; adopted without OSMP-specific definition.

column5: Difference / spacing / offset parameter; replaces word DELTA in slot names
Origin or home position reference; replaces word HOME
Abort condition trigger or comms-denial state designator
Time constant or timeout duration; used in N:CFG and Overflow Protocol config syntax; lowercase Greek
Set membership; scope or permission boundary declaration
Set minus; missing fragment indicator in A:NACK payloads

column1: CATEGORY 5 — LOSS TOLERANCE POLICY DESIGNATORS (UTIL Section X-C Cat.5; replaces legacy codes FS/GD/AT)
Greek uppercase letters whose pre-existing mathematical meanings map directly to Overflow Protocol policy semantics.

column7: Fundamental invariant — silent discard on incomplete receipt; policy holds regardless of what arrives
Graduated threshold response — execute maximum satisfiable subset of received fragments; default policy
Indivisible function — no fragment executes unless all fragments received within timeout window; on incomplete receipt transmit A:NACK

column1: CATEGORY 6 — DICTIONARY UPDATE MODE DESIGNATORS (UTIL Section X-C Cat.6; replaces words ADDITIVE/REPLACE/DEPRECATE)
Pre-existing standard typography and arithmetic meanings; used exclusively in dictionary delta payload mode fields.

column5: New entry appended; existing entries preserved; version pointer incremented; graceful degradation transmission class
New entry supersedes existing; prior entry retired; mandatory retransmit on packet loss (FLAGS[C]); mandatory retransmission transmission class
Entry marked retired; semantic resolution preserved for backward compatibility; graceful degradation transmission class

column1: SECTION 2: SLOT VALUE ENCODING TABLE (UTIL Single-Character Conformance Rule)

Rule: All OSMP-defined enumerated slot value sets with finite protocol-specified cardinality MUST be encoded as a single uppercase Latin letter (A-Z) or single decimal digit (0-9). Slot values drawn from external open-ended domain registries (ICD-10 codes
column4: model identifiers) are encoded as their native representation within slot brackets — exempt from this rule.

column1: Source columns: Namespace | Prefix | Domain | Opcode | Definition | Layer | Authoritative_Source | UTIL_Claim_Ref | floor_version

Note: Layer 1 = OSMP-defined semantic primitive (resolves by ASD lookup). Layer 2 = accessor pattern into external open-ended registry (slot value drawn from external standard; exempt from single-character slot value rule).

column5: positive_acknowledgment — complement to NACK; used in Atomic policy confirmation for K and H namespace instructions
asd_version_identity_or_delta — ADP version exchange and delta synchronization; sub-instructions A:ASD:REQ A:ASD:DELTA A:ASD:DEF A:ASD:HASH
compress_compare — context compression and comparison utility (see Dataset C example)
compliance_gate_assertion — compliance check precondition in cross-namespace authorization chain I:KYC∧I:AML→I:⊤→A:COMP→K:TRD→R:⚠MOV
structured_comparison_returning_result — agent output or state comparison producing a returnable result value

registered_macro_invocation — invoke a pre-validated multi-step SAL instruction chain template by name and slot-fill; the macro identifier names a chain template registered in the ASD; the agent retrieves the template and substitutes context-specific slot values without composing from individual opcodes; compact mode transmits macro identifier and slot fills only (receiver expands from local ASD); expanded mode transmits the fully expanded chain; see OSMP-SPEC Section 11
mdr_corpus_version_identity_or_delta — ADP corpus version exchange; sub-instructions A:MDR:REQ
negative_acknowledgment — used in Atomic policy; payload encodes missing fragment indices as A:NACK[MSG:[id]∖[missing_indices]]
propose_action_for_negotiation — coordination primitive decoded by ASD lookup; NOT a FIPA-ACL performative; communicative structure carried by glyph operators not by opcode type
permission_grant — agent permission assertion; measurement-validated sibling of A:AUTH authorization_assertion
comply_with_proposed_action — commitment to execute a proposed action; mid-gradient between A:ACK (weakest) and A:ACCEPT (strongest)
column7: OSMP-defined; consent-gradient framing from LLM-as-judge literature
